concepts.name: Bisection Bandwidth Wall
concepts.description: The scaling ceiling where adding hardware degrades performance because network synchronization overhead exceeds computational gains.

concepts.name: Communication-Computation Ratio
concepts.description: The ratio of time spent moving data across the network versus performing arithmetic operations on the accelerators.

concepts.name: Compute-Bound
concepts.description: A system state where accelerators spend the majority of their time multiplying matrices rather than waiting for data transfers.

concepts.name: Communication-Bound
concepts.description: A system state where network synchronization saturates bandwidth, leaving accelerators idle while waiting for parameters or gradients.

concepts.name: Data Parallelism
concepts.description: A strategy that splits the training dataset across multiple devices while replicating the entire model on each device.

concepts.name: Tensor Parallelism
concepts.description: A strategy that vectorizes inner loops of matrix multiplication across intra-node GPUs to distribute memory and compute.

concepts.name: Pipeline Parallelism
concepts.description: A strategy that partitions sequential model layers across different nodes to minimize inter-node communication volume.

concepts.name: 3D Parallelism
concepts.description: The simultaneous combination of data, tensor, and pipeline parallelism to map massive models onto a distributed hardware fleet.

concepts.name: Pipeline Bubble
concepts.description: The idle compute time GPUs spend waiting for activations or gradients to propagate through the stages of a pipeline.

concepts.name: ZeRO Optimizations
concepts.description: Memory efficiency techniques that partition model states to prevent data parallelism from failing on large parameter counts.

concepts.name: All-Reduce
concepts.description: A collective communication operation that aggregates and synchronizes computed gradients across all independent devices.

concepts.name: Ring All-Reduce
concepts.description: A bandwidth-efficient implementation of gradient synchronization that passes data chunks in a logical ring topology.

concepts.name: Gradient Accumulation
concepts.description: A technique that processes multiple microbatches locally before synchronizing, reducing the frequency of communication overhead.

concepts.name: Model FLOPs Utilization
concepts.description: A metric that quantifies the percentage of peak theoretical hardware performance actively used during distributed training.

concepts.name: Young-Daly Formula
concepts.description: A mathematical equation used to calculate the optimal checkpointing frequency by balancing save overhead against potential rework time.
